title: Tiruvallur Ammonia Leak Death Toll Rises to Five; Factory Owner Arrested
summary: An ammonia gas leak at a seafood processing unit in Tiruvallur, Tamil Nadu, has killed five workers and hospitalised 67 others. Tamil Nadu CM Vijay has ordered a three-member inquiry into the incident, and police have arrested the factory owner along with members of management.

coverages.headline: Tiruvallur ammonia leak death toll rises to five; TN govt orders probe
coverages.excerpt: An ammonia leak at a seafood processing unit in Tiruvallur, Tamil Nadu, killed five workers and hospitalised 67 others; CM Vijay ordered a three-member inquiry and police arrested the factory owner and management.
coverages.bias_notes: The arrest of the factory owner and management alongside the inquiry order suggests this is being treated with unusual speed and seriousness compared to many industrial accident cases in India, where prosecutions often lag well behind the incident itself or don't happen at all. The 67 hospitalised figure, more than ten times the death toll, indicates a much wider exposure event than the fatality count alone conveys — ammonia gas leaks can cause serious respiratory injury that doesn't register as a death but represents significant ongoing harm. The piece doesn't address what safety violations, if any, are alleged, or whether this seafood processing unit had a prior inspection or compliance history — context that would clarify whether this was a foreseeable risk or a genuine accident.
